# Iris

> A portable runtime for durable AI agents. You declare an agent as a config file plus
> a folder (instructions, tools, skills); `iris build` compiles it into an open,
> content-addressed image you run anywhere. At runtime a session is an event-sourced
> journal — so it is durable (a crash loses nothing) and portable (pause on one host,
> resume on another, byte-for-byte identical), against any LLM, over any channel.

## Getting started
- [Introduction](docs/introduction.md): what Iris is, and the one idea — a session is a journal.
- [Your first agent](docs/first-agent.md): init → build → chat with no API key, then a real model; resume across a restart.
- [Tools](docs/tools.md): tools as versioned contracts referenced by address; the tool boundary; secrets & environment.
- [Channels](docs/channels.md): durable, resumable sessions over HTTP (SSE/WebSocket), the web chat UI, the client SDK.
- [Deploy](docs/deploy.md): resume the same session on a different host — one command to a Cloudflare Durable Object.

## Concepts
- [The harness](docs/harness.md): the model↔tool loop as swappable tactics at five seams (assembleContext, shouldCompact, decideNext, gateAction, onToolError); every decision is journaled, so tactics can be third-party/nondeterministic and replay still can't diverge. Default bundle + the coding bundle; configure via harness.bundle / harness.tactics.
- [Models & providers](docs/providers.md): vendor-neutral, replay-safe model adapters; how to add one.
- [Governance & approvals](docs/governance.md): the approval gate, the policy layer, the journaled approval trail.
- [Audit & reproducible evals](docs/audit-and-evals.md): iris audit; reproducible evals; subagents & schedules.
- [Verifiable portable journals](docs/verifiable-journal.md): content-addressed *.irisjournal export/verify/import.

## Guides
- [Subagents](docs/guides/subagents.md): durable delegation to a child agent (own session); the child's output is journaled in the parent, which replays without re-running it. Configure via subagents.json.
- [Schedules](docs/guides/schedules.md): recurring jobs as durable replayable agents; each cycle parks on a timer; a host-side pump resumes due ones (iris schedule).
- [Observability](docs/guides/observability.md): OTel-shaped spans derived from the journal (@irisrun/observe), injected Sink, env-gated OTLP export; derived so replay is unaffected.
- [Inspecting a session](docs/guides/inspect.md): inspectSession renders the deterministic decision/effect/marker timeline of a recorded session (a library API, not an iris subcommand; iris inspect is image-only).
- [Frontend & the client SDK](docs/guides/frontend.md): the web chat UI (iris serve --web) and the isomorphic @irisrun/client-sdk over the serve SSE two-identifier protocol.
- [Secrets & environment](docs/guides/secrets.md): declare secret names in the Agentfile, supply values at run time (--env-file / --secret-files); least-privilege tool env.

## Reference
- [Agentfile reference](docs/reference/agentfile.md): every Agentfile field, the JSON form, and the build-time validation rules.
- [CLI](docs/reference/cli.md): every iris command and its flags.
- [Harness seams](docs/reference/harness-seams.md): the normative tactic/bundle contract — seam signatures, composition, the journaled effect, invariant caps.
- [Channel-port spec](docs/reference/channel-port-spec.md): the contract every channel passes.
- [Bridge pattern](docs/reference/bridge-pattern.md): Discord/Telegram/Teams as external bridges.
- [Verifiable-journal format](docs/reference/verifiable-journal-spec.md): the export format, reproducible from the file alone.
- [Journal threat model](docs/reference/threat-model.md): what journal verification detects, and what it does not claim.
- [Sandbox-egress threat model](docs/reference/security-sandbox-threat-model.md): the egress firewall + credential broker.

## Contributing
- [CONTRIBUTING](CONTRIBUTING.md): the dev loop (install-free; npm test / typecheck), the CI gate (npm ci → typecheck → test), and the house rules.
- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md): the pure core + the two host ports (StateStore/Scheduler) + the adapter layers (how the ~29 packages fit).
- [Conventions](docs/conventions.md): enforced house rules — core-is-pure (relative imports only), zero-dep, loud-refusal/no-silent-failure, tests-first.
- [Add a provider](docs/contributing/adding-a-provider.md) / [channel](docs/contributing/adding-a-channel.md) / [store](docs/contributing/adding-a-store.md) / [tactic](docs/contributing/adding-a-tactic.md): the extension recipes, each with a conformance suite.
- **Adapter SDK** — `@irisrun/sdk` packages the port types + conformance suites + the `openStore`/`openModelProvider`/`openChannel` contracts into ONE dependency; `iris adapter init <kind> <name>` scaffolds a conformance-wired package, and `--store`/`--provider`/`--channel <module>` load it without forking the CLI.

## Discovery
- [agents.md](agents.md): how an AI agent or tool integrates with Iris — Agentfile schema, MCP server, REST API.
- [README](README.md): the project manifesto (why Iris exists).
